data.description:
## What
Surfaced by the OSS-best-practices validation of the config epic (a621). The typed-config precedence stack is documented and implemented as CLI > env > project > user > defaults, and `load_config(cli_overrides=...)` / `resolve_with_sources` / `_ordered_layers` fully support a `cli` layer — but NO first-class CLI flag currently routes through `cli_overrides`. So the top layer of the documented precedence is reachable only by library callers, never by the `rebar` CLI, and `rebar config` advertises a `cli` source that the CLI itself can never produce.

This is an incomplete-feature gap, not a correctness bug (precedence is correct when cli_overrides IS passed; library callers can use it today). OSS tools (pip/uv/poetry/ruff) all let CLI flags override env+files.

## Scope / Deliverables
- Pick the config keys that warrant a CLI flag (candidates: --push/--no-pull already exist as ad-hoc flags in EV-1; --threshold in compact; a generic --config-set k=v is an option) and route them into load_config via cli_overrides at the relevant CLI arms, so a flag beats env+file+default.
- OR, if no CLI flags are wanted yet, keep the cli layer as a documented library-only override and adjust `rebar config`'s header/docs so it does not imply a CLI-populated layer that cannot occur.
- Tests asserting CLI flag > env > file precedence end-to-end.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] At least one real CLI flag routes through cli_overrides and demonstrably beats env+file (test), OR the cli layer is explicitly documented as library-only and the transparency header no longer implies otherwise
- [ ] `rebar config` provenance shows `cli` for a flag-set value (if flags are wired)
- [ ] Full non-integration suite green
data.title: Wire CLI flags through load_config(cli_overrides) — documented CLI precedence layer is unwired
